title: Work-order condition satisfied state
definition: The visible existing-condition state selected when the native cached next-check result is true.

facts.statement: A true cached result renders the annotation Satisfied for next check in bright native green foreground index 2 on black background index 0.
The satisfied attributes apply to the next-check annotation, not the row's operator, quantity, or item-description fragments.

uncertainties.statement: The pinned corpus does not capture this condition with a true cached result.

positive: A true cached result displays the exact satisfied annotation and all three color attributes.
boundary: Only the annotation span is bright green; adjacent condition fragments retain their own attributes.
counterexamples: The same row with a false cached result uses the unsatisfied state instead.

absenceGuards: Do not infer this state from the underlying inventory condition when the native cached result is unavailable.

requiredFrames: Existing row with a true cached result and the complete next-check annotation span
